source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Unauthorized Discharge of Synthetic Oil Based Mud

event.top_event: Unauthorized discharge of synthetic oil based mud overboard
actions_taken: All dump valves closed at 1105 hours
BSEE Inspectors arrived on location on 18 February 2015 to investigate
Permit to Work and Risk Assessment documents reviewed
event.summary: On 13 February 2015 at 1115 hours, a 260 barrel discharge of 15 ppg Synthetic Oil Based Mud (SOBM) occurred at MC 762 on the Stena Forth. The Derrickman intended to transfer salt water from active pit #2 to active pit #4 but was unaware that SOBM from equalized active pits #1 and #3 had flowed into active pit #2 due to failure of the air actuator for active pit #2 and the suction valve for active pit #3. Poor communication between the Derrickman and Mud Loggers, combined with equipment failure and lack of continuous pit monitoring, resulted in the unauthorized discharge.
recommendations: Implement continuous monitoring of pit volumes during all fluid transfer operations
Establish robust communication protocols between Derrickman, Mud Loggers, and Driller
Conduct regular maintenance and testing of air actuators and suction valves
Require verification of pit contents before initiating transfers
Ensure personnel remain in the Mud Control Room during active pit operations

hazards.name: Presence of synthetic oil based mud in active mud pits
Operation of master dump valve and pit transfer systems

threats.name: Air actuator failure allowing unintended valve opening
Suction valve failure allowing mud to flow between pits
Poor communication between Derrickman and Mud Loggers
Lack of continuous pit volume monitoring
Derrickman unaware of SOBM presence in active pit #2

consequences.name: Discharge of 260 barrels of synthetic oil based mud to offshore waters
Environmental pollution of Gulf of Mexico

controls.name: Permit to Work and Risk Assessment documentation
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control requiring continuous pit monitoring
controls.lod_basis: Procedural requirement for safe pit operations

supporting_text: both documents clearly state continuous monitoring of pits or volumes
The pits were left unattended when the Permit to Work and the Risk Assessment clearly state that the pits should be monitored continuously

controls.name: Air actuator for active pit #2
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to prevent unintended valve opening
controls.lod_basis: Mechanical device to control pit valve operation

supporting_text: the air actuator for active pit #2 and the suction valve for active pit #3 failed
an air actuated valve for #3 pit failed, allowing the valve to partially open at 1037 hours

controls.name: Suction valve for active pit #3
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to prevent mud flow between pits
controls.lod_basis: Mechanical valve to isolate pit contents

supporting_text: the air actuator for active pit #2 and the suction valve for active pit #3 failed
This allowed the valve to partially open and SOBM to flow from active pit #3 into active pit #2

controls.name: Pit Volume Totalizer (PVT) monitoring system
controls.barrier_role: Detection system to identify abnormal pit volume changes
controls.lod_basis: Real-time monitoring to detect equipment failures and unauthorized transfers

supporting_text: The logs from the Pit Volume Totalizer (PVT) indicated that active pit #1 and active pit #3 containing SOBM, were equalized
the Mud Logger on duty noticed active pits #1 and #3 were losing mud at 1038 hours
According to the screenshot taken from the Mud Loggers pit monitoring system, active pit #2 was clearly gaining fluid while active #1 and #3 were losing fluid

controls.name: Mud Logger pit volume monitoring and communication
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control for continuous monitoring and alerting
controls.lod_basis: Personnel assigned to monitor pit volumes and communicate anomalies

supporting_text: Poor communication between the Derrickman and the Mud Loggers
The Mud Logger called the MCR two or three times with no answer
At this time no personnel were monitoring pit volumes
After reviewing the Derrickman's statement he was not informed or aware that active pits #1 and #3 were losing mud

controls.name: Low limit alarm in active pit #3
controls.barrier_role: Detection system to alert to abnormal pit levels
controls.lod_basis: Automated alarm to detect loss of mud from pit

supporting_text: At approximately 1057 hours, the Derrickman observed a low limit alarm in active pit #3 containing 15 ppg SOBM, and stopped the transfer from active pit #2 to active pit #4

controls.name: Derrickman verification of pit contents before transfer
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to verify fluid identity before transfer
controls.lod_basis: Procedural requirement to prevent transfer of wrong fluid

supporting_text: The Derrickman did not check the pit levels upon returning to the MCR
the Derrickman thought he was transferring salt water from acive pit #2 pit to active #4 pit
The Derrickman was unaware that SOBM, from equalized active pits #1 and #3 had flowed into active pit #2

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
